headline_summary: Iran threatens to attack any ship attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.
Five ships have been struck in a series of missile and drone attacks in the region.
Gulf states are considering military responses to Iranian aggression.
Shipping companies are rerouting vessels around Africa due to safety concerns.
Oil prices have surged amid fears of supply disruptions.
The Strait of Hormuz is critical for global oil and gas supplies.
Insurers are reassessing coverage for transits through the strait.
The situation has escalated tensions in the Middle East significantly.

Energy: Oil prices expected to rise sharply due to supply fears.
Natural gas prices have already increased significantly in Europe and Asia.
Potential for historic upheaval in crude tanker markets.
FX: Increased demand for safe-haven currencies like the USD.
Emerging market currencies may face depreciation pressures.

risk_drivers: Escalation of military conflict in the Gulf region.
Disruption of global oil and gas supplies.
Increased geopolitical tensions affecting trade routes.

watchlist.why: Expected price surge due to supply disruptions.
